title: Check the Tote Error Field In the Tote Task Interface
summary: Use the Tote Task interface to determine whether a tote-related error is shown for a tote and capture the associated tote and task identifiers displayed on the same record.
when_to_use: Use when investigating a tote and you need to verify whether the Tote Task interface shows a tote-related error and identify the associated Tote ID, Task Name, Task ID, and sorter task UID if shown.
not_for: Interpreting the meaning of a tote error when the source does not define the error.
Resolving or correcting tote errors; this source only supports checking and recording what is displayed.
Using fields not shown or supported by the Tote Task interface evidence in this source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open or view the Tote Task interface, inspect the Tote Error field, and record the displayed tote and task identifiers exactly as shown.

safety_notes: This source supports a diagnostic information-gathering check only.
Do not infer corrective actions, error meanings, or system changes from this source alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Tote Task interface
Visibility of the Tote Error, Tote ID, Task ID, and Task Name fields

access_required: Access to the Tote Task interface
system_preconditions: The tote record to be investigated is visible in the Tote Task interface.
The Tote Error and related tote/task fields are visible on the displayed record.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the Tote Tasks Aveva API page and fields including Tote ID, Task ID, Task Name, and Tote Error.

steps.title: Open or view the Tote Task interface
steps.purpose: Access the interface that displays tote task details and the Tote Error field.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Tote Task interface for the tote being investigated.
steps.expected_result: The Tote Task interface is visible for the tote record under investigation.
steps.healthy_condition: The interface displays tote task details for the relevant tote record.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote Task interface cannot be opened or the relevant tote record is not visible.

actions.label: Open Tote Task interface
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Tote Task interface for the tote being investigated.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote Tasks Aveva API page showing the tote task detail fields, including Tote Error.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Tote Task interface is unavailable.
The tote being investigated cannot be matched to a visible record.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide title appears as Tote Tasks Aveva API page. The slide describes the Tote Task interface for insight into tote movements within the warehouse.

steps.title: Locate the Tote Error field
steps.purpose: Identify the specific field used to show tote-related error information.
steps.instruction: Locate the Tote Error field on the interface.
steps.expected_result: The Tote Error field is identified on the displayed Tote Task record.
steps.healthy_condition: A field labeled Tote Error is visible on the interface.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote Error field is missing, unreadable, or cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Find Tote Error field
actions.exact_action: Locate the Tote Error field on the interface.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote Error field listed among the Tote Task interface data fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Tote Error field is missing.
The Tote Error field is unreadable.
The displayed fields do not align with the expected Tote Task record.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The interface includes a Tote Error field; the transcript also references checking if there is any error.
Tote Task interface includes a Tote Error field for viewing tote-related error information alongside tote and task identifiers.

steps.title: Check whether an error is shown
steps.purpose: Determine whether the interface currently indicates a tote-related error for the displayed record.
steps.instruction: Check whether any error is shown in the Tote Error field.
steps.expected_result: You can confirm whether the Tote Error field shows an error indication or not.
steps.healthy_condition: The Tote Error field is readable and its current state can be clearly observed.
steps.failure_condition: The field content is unclear, unreadable, or appears inconsistent with the displayed tote record.

actions.label: Inspect Tote Error value
actions.exact_action: Check whether any error is shown in the Tote Error field.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote Error field value for the displayed tote task record.

stop_or_escalate_if: An error is shown but the source does not provide enough information to interpret or resolve it.
The Tote Error field is unreadable.
The Tote Error field does not align with the displayed tote record.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The interface includes a Tote Error field; the transcript also references checking if there is any error.

steps.title: Read the Tote ID
steps.purpose: Capture the tote identifier associated with the displayed error state.
steps.instruction: Read the Tote ID shown for the same record.
steps.expected_result: The Tote ID for the displayed record is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: A Tote ID is visible and can be associated with the same record as the Tote Error field.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote ID is missing, unreadable, or does not appear to belong to the same displayed record.

actions.label: Read Tote ID
actions.exact_action: Read the Tote ID shown for the same record.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote ID field on the same Tote Task record as the Tote Error field.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Tote ID is missing or unreadable.
The Tote ID cannot be confidently tied to the same record as the Tote Error field.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Fields shown include Tote ID, AGV ID, Original Destination, Station, Task ID, Task Name, and Tote Error; transcript also mentions sorter task UID.
Tote Task interface fields include Tote ID, AGV ID, Original Destination, Station, Task ID, Task Name, Tote Error, and sorter task UID references.

steps.title: Review task identifiers
steps.purpose: Associate the displayed tote error state with the task information shown on the same record.
steps.instruction: Review the Task Name and Task ID fields, and sorter task UID if shown, to associate the error with the displayed task.
steps.expected_result: The task identifiers associated with the displayed tote record are identified.
steps.healthy_condition: Task Name and Task ID are visible, and sorter task UID is captured if it is shown.
steps.failure_condition: Task Name, Task ID, or sorter task UID are missing, unreadable, or cannot be associated with the same record.

actions.label: Review task fields
actions.exact_action: Review the Task Name and Task ID fields, and sorter task UID if shown, to associate the error with the displayed task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Task ID, Task Name, and any sorter task UID information shown with the Tote Task record.

stop_or_escalate_if: Task Name or Task ID is missing or unreadable.
Sorter task UID is expected by the workflow but is not shown.
The task fields do not appear to match the displayed tote record.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Fields shown include Tote ID, AGV ID, Original Destination, Station, Task ID, Task Name, and Tote Error; transcript also mentions sorter task UID.
The aligned transcript adds that users can view current task details ... what station it is at, sorter task UID, pickup tote task name, and any error information.

steps.title: Record the displayed values exactly as shown
steps.purpose: Preserve the observed error state and associated identifiers for follow-up or escalation.
steps.instruction: Record the displayed error indication and the related tote and task identifiers exactly as shown.
steps.expected_result: A complete record of the displayed Tote Error state and associated identifiers is captured.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded details match the interface exactly and include the visible tote and task identifiers.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded details are incomplete, ambiguous, or do not match the displayed interface.

actions.label: Document displayed values
actions.exact_action: Record the displayed error indication and the related tote and task identifiers exactly as shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: All displayed Tote Task fields needed to document the error indication and associated identifiers.

stop_or_escalate_if: An error is shown but the source does not provide enough information to interpret or resolve it.
The displayed values cannot be read clearly enough to document exactly.
The Tote Error field is missing, unreadable, or does not align with the displayed tote record.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Fields shown include Tote ID, AGV ID, Original Destination, Station, Task ID, Task Name, and Tote Error; transcript also mentions sorter task UID.

success_criteria: The Tote Task interface is viewed for the tote under investigation.
The Tote Error field is located and checked.
The user confirms whether an error is shown.
The displayed Tote ID, Task Name, Task ID, and sorter task UID if shown are captured exactly as displayed.
healthy_conditions: The Tote Task record is visible and readable.
The Tote Error field is present and readable.
The Tote ID and task-related fields are visible on the same record.
The observed values can be documented exactly as shown.
failure_conditions: The Tote Task interface cannot be accessed or the relevant record cannot be viewed.
The Tote Error field is missing or unreadable.
The Tote Error field does not align with the displayed tote record.
The tote or task identifiers are missing, unreadable, or cannot be associated with the same record.
An error is shown but the source does not provide enough information to interpret or resolve it.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if an error is shown but the source does not provide enough information to interpret or resolve it.
Escalate if the Tote Error field is missing, unreadable, or does not align with the displayed tote record.
